what_it_does: Renders a persisted `payload.context[]` notebook for a build intent: a sorted, navigable list of attached strata (comments, chats, history, versions) with an optimistic pin toggle and fail-loud error reporting.
observations: The displayed context is treated as a frozen consent-time snapshot; the component does not re-resolve it.
Pin state is kept in a local optimistic overlay keyed by item timestamp; the backend write affects future gathers, not this bundle.
Items are sorted newest-first by `ts` using locale-aware string comparison.
Addresses are only clickable when they start with `ui://`; `run://` addresses are shown as static labels to avoid mis-routing.
`kindFace` maps known R2 stratum kinds to monospace glyphs and short labels, falling back to a generic bullet.
The empty state explicitly states that no attached context exists instead of rendering a blank panel.
CSS classes (`cb-*`) and `data-ui-ref` attributes suggest a token-based design system with no hardcoded colors in the file.
Errors from pinning are surfaced in a dedicated `cb-err` element with a fail-loud message.
standouts: Explicit frozen-snapshot semantics: the view is the exact context the build prompt will see at mint/approve time.
Optimistic pin overlay on an immutable bundle, clearly separating local reflection from persisted state.
Navigation guard that disables address links for non-`ui://` schemes, preventing accidental camera-path mis-routes.
conventions: Async event handler with explicit `busy` flag and `try/catch/finally`.
Early-return empty-state branch before the main render.
Heavy use of `title` attributes for inline context and `data-ui-ref` for UI registry markers.
CSS-class naming tied to component prefix (`cb-*`) rather than utility classes.
Fail-loud error handling: errors are set and displayed, not swallowed.
concerns: Broad `any` typing for `d`, items, and the `api.pin` response reduces compile-time safety.
Optimistic overlay keyed by `ts` could collide if two context items share the same timestamp.
Only one global `busyTs` state means concurrent pin toggles on different items would show a single spinner.
`relTime` and `api.pin` are imported but their contracts are not visible in this file.
No cleanup or reset of `err` after a successful navigation or timeout; the error remains until the next pin action.
notes: A presentation-only context panel that exposes what a build will see, lets operators hold items with an optimistic pin, and carefully restricts navigation to `ui://` addresses.
questions: What is the exact shape of `payload.context` items and the `api.pin` response?
How are `run://` addresses intended to be resolved if not through `onNavigate`?
What does the `data-needs-tim="x5-context-bundle"` attribute trigger in the surrounding system?
Where is `resolveUiTarget` implemented and how does it validate `ui://` targets?
Is `ts` guaranteed unique within a bundle, or should the overlay key combine `address` and `ts`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it surfaces the persisted context bundle, supports navigation for `ui://` addresses, and provides an optimistic pin control with fail-loud feedback.
apparent_themes: Consent-time immutability and trust in surfaced context
Optimistic local UI with clear backend-state separation
intention_signals: The file appears designed to make the build's context notebook transparent and operator-controllable: show exactly what was frozen at mint time, let the operator pin items to keep them in future gathers, and prevent navigation mistakes by gating address links to the `ui://` registry.
novelty: The combination of an immutable consent-time context snapshot with an optimistic local pin overlay, plus an explicit scheme-guard that renders `run://` addresses non-navigable, is a distinctive design choice.
contribution: It contributes a trustworthy, read-mostly context inspector with a safe pin affordance and namespace-aware navigation, preserving the property that the displayed bundle equals the build's prompt context.
summary_for_embedding: A React component that renders a build intent's frozen `payload.context` list, newest first, with kind glyphs, `ui://` navigation, optimistic pin toggles, and fail-loud pin errors.
intention_for_embedding: To give operators a transparent, consent-time view of the attached strata a build will see and let them hold selected items in the bounded R2 context window.

symbols.does: Maps a stratum kind string to a display glyph and short label.
symbols.description: Normalizes the input kind and returns a monospace glyph and readable label for known R2 strata such as annotation/comment, chat, history, version, self/change, with a fallback bullet for unrecognized kinds.

symbols.does: Renders the persisted context bundle with sorting, navigation, and pin controls.
symbols.description: Accepts a build-intent object `d` and an optional `onNavigate` callback, displays the `payload.context` items newest-first, renders each item's kind, address, relative timestamp, text, and an optimistic pin button, and only enables address navigation for `ui://` targets.

symbols.does: Toggles the pinned state of a context item via `api.pin` with optimistic local reflection.
symbols.description: Validates that the item has both `ts` and `address`, computes the next pin state, calls the backend pin endpoint, and on success updates a local overlay; on failure it sets an error message and leaves the persisted snapshot unchanged.
